justifications.ip1: All three ACs have distinct, specific failing test scenarios: AC1 covered by T1 ('expect(result).toEqual({ blocked: true, blockedBy: "RBNZ_SANCTIONED" })') and T2 (AUSTRAC not called), AC2 by T3, and AC3 by T4 and T5 with precise assertions on the returned shape and audit log content.
justifications.ip2: The plan creates only the two files specified in the DoR ('src/aml/dual-aml-screener.js' and 'tests/aml/dual-aml-screener.test.js'), explicitly lists routing, SWIFT notification, AUSTRAC reporting, FX reporting, and DIA registration as NOT implemented, and no task touches any of those out-of-scope items.
justifications.ip3: Every task names specific files, functions, and concrete assertions — e.g. Task 1 names 'tests/aml/dual-aml-screener.test.js' with line-level mock setup and exact expect calls; Task 2 names 'src/aml/dual-aml-screener.js' with the full implementation; Task 3 provides a grep-based static checklist tied to exact patterns.
justifications.ip4: Task 1 writes all 8 failing tests first and explicitly states 'All tests must fail at this point because src/aml/dual-aml-screener.js does not yet exist' (confirming RED), and Task 2 writes the implementation only after, with 'Expected state after Task 2: All 8 tests pass (GREEN)' confirming GREEN.
justifications.ip5: NFR-1/C7 sequential ordering is enforced by T6 (call-order array test that would catch Promise.all) and by code using sequential awaits; NFR-2/C8 audit logging on every path is enforced by T5, T7, and T8 with specific assertions including 'austracResult: null' on RBNZ early exit, and C8 console prohibition is enforced by the Task 3 grep checklist.

notes: Exemplary plan: all ACs have precise failing test scenarios, C7 sequential ordering is enforced by a call-order test that explicitly catches Promise.all, NFR-2 audit logging is tested in all three outcome paths, and no out-of-scope work appears anywhere in the plan.
